channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Khosla Ventures is betting $10M on Ian Crosby, whose last startup, Bench, imploded
item.description: Crosby is building Synthetic, a fully autonomous AI bookkeeping service for other startups.

item.title: The Beats Solo 4 have returned to their best price of the year
item.description: If you want a pair of Apple-made wireless headphones that work just as well with Android as with iOS, the Beats Solo 4 are down to around $129.95 ($70 off) at Amazon, Best Buy, and Target. That matches the best price we’ve seen so far this year. While each retailer is offering discounts on the […]

item.title: Watch the World Cup in 4K glory with the Hisense U7 — now over $500 off this excellent Mini-LED TV at Amazon
item.description: Grab the Hisense U7SG for just $998.99 at Amazon.

item.title: AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon join forces to eliminate mobile coverage dead zones
item.description: The rival carriers will pool resources to improve satellite capacity and boost connectivity in underserved areas.

item.title: How to turn off Instagram’s new Instants feature and retract photos you accidentally shared
item.description: While the Meta-owned social network billed Instants as a new format to share real-life moments as they’re happening, many users are looking for a way to turn off the feature, especially those who have ended up accidentally sending images to others not fully understanding how the feature works.

item.title: Razer’s Blade 18 is getting a $500 price hike and a new Intel chip
item.description: Razer just announced a new Blade 18 gaming laptop with an Intel Core Ultra 9 290HX Plus chip. The flagship laptop is available now on Razer's site, starting at $3,999.99 with an Nvidia RTX 5070 Ti and configurable with up to an RTX 5090. That starting config is $500 more than the 2025 model with […]

item.title: Microsoft's Copilot can now peek into your open tabs in Edge — if you let it — as part of new AI features for the browser
item.description: Microsoft has ditched Copilot Mode from Edge — but if you're cheering that move, you won't like what's replaced it.

item.title: iOS 26.5 updates unlocks Apple Watch and AirPod features for third-party alternatives — but only if you live in the EU
item.description: Apple is finally bringing Apple Watch and AirPod features to third-party wearables.

item.title: Smart Band 10 Pro : Xiaomi montre déjà son futur bracelet premium (et il change tout)
item.description: Près d'un an après le lancement de son Smart Band 10, Xiaomi a commencé à teaser l'arrivée, en Chine, d'une version plus haute en gamme, le futur Xiaomi Smart Band 10 Pro.

item.title: Apple vole au secours de Google : l’alliance inédite contre Bruxelles sur l’IA
item.description: Apple a officiellement pris la défense de Google contre les projets européens qui forceraient Android à s'ouvrir aux assistants IA concurrents. Quand les deux plus grands rivaux de la tech s'allient, c'est rarement par altruisme.

item.title: Home Depot's 2026 Memorial Day sale just went live — here's everything I'm buying for my backyard
item.description: Home Depot's 2026 Memorial Day sale is live, and I'm rounding up my favorite deals for a backyard refresh, including 40% off patio furniture, grills, flowers, tools, and more.

item.title: How to watch Colors outside India – from anywhere with a VPN
item.description: Here's how to watch Colors from outside India, and learn how to access streams from anywhere abroad with a VPN.

item.title: AI scraping has become its own media business
item.description: A shadow industry of data middlemen is turning publishers’ work into fuel for AI agents—and forcing media companies to rethink what “outputs” really mean There are several dimensions to the ongoing legal war between the media industry and AI companies over copyright, and one of the major ones is the question of outputs. Which is to say: Scraping content without permission may be detestable, but if the party doing the scraping isn’t doing anything with it that would compete with the content creator, it’s difficult to prove harm. And many legal proceedings, especially civil claims, depend on sho...

item.title: How companies weaponize the terms of service against you
item.description: Today, I’m talking with Brendan Ballou, founder of the Public Integrity Project and author of a new book, called When Companies Run the Courts, about the rise of forced arbitration. Brendan’s actually been on the show before — his previous book, Plunder, was about private equity taking over huge swaths of American life, and that conversation […]

item.title: Margo's Got Money Troubles season 2 has been confirmed by Apple TV — here's why you shouldn't miss this binge worthy comedy series
item.description: Margo's Got Money Troubles will return for season 2 and it's the best news I've heard all week.

item.title: Spider-Man: Brand New Day gets a fresh poster that sparks Marvel fan hopes over a new trailer release — but some can't stop making fun of the artwork's accompanying caption
item.description: Marvel fans are mocking Sony over the release of a fresh Spider-Man: Brand New Day poster.

item.title: Southeast Asia’s AI boom has a power problem — and it’s being underestimated
item.description: Southeast Asia’s AI surge is accelerating but power constraints may define its limits.

item.title: Steam Frame : une deuxième version moins chère du casque VR ? Cet indice est prometteur
item.description: Certaines données laissent penser que Valve préparerait deux versions de son casque VR Steam Frame. L'une d'elles pourrait logiquement être vendue moins cher.

item.title: Regularly $2,499.99, the 65-inch Samsung OLED 4K S90F TV is over $1,000 off if you act fast
item.description: The Samsung 65-inch S90F OLED 4K TV is on sale at Amazon for only $1,397.99. That's a 44% price drop from its original list price.

item.title: A spyware investigator exposed Russian government hackers trying to hijack Signal accounts
item.description: A group of likely Russian government hackers tried to hack a security researcher who investigates spyware attacks. He was then able to turn the tables on the hackers and reveal details of their espionage campaign.

item.title: AMD’s FSR 4.1 upscaling tech is coming to older graphics cards
item.description: AMD is bringing its FSR 4.1 upscaling tech to older graphics cards. In an announcement on X, Jack Huynh, AMD's head of computing and graphics, says FSR 4.1 will roll out to RDNA 3 GPUs in July, followed by a launch on RDNA 2 chips in early 2027. This summer's update will allow Radeon RX […]

item.title: Normally $1,499.99, the Roborock Qrevo CurvX robot vacuum hits best-ever price of $899.98 at Amazon
item.description: The Roborock Qrevo CurvX robot vacuum is on sale at Amazon for $899.98, down from the normal price of $1,499.99. That's a 40% discount.

item.title: Identity breaches are on the rise, and are only getting worse - so how can your business stay safe?
item.description: A unified, automated identity security approach is needed, CyberArk says as it warns about AI agents' access to sensitive files.

item.title: With Saros demanding more complex button mapping, I might finally upgrade to the Dualsense Edge at this record-low price
item.description: It's hard to justify the Dualsense Edge at full price, but I'm tempted to pick one up for demanding games like Marathon and Saros now that it's dropped to a stunning low price.

item.title: Cisco cuts nearly 4,000 jobs to spend more on AI, reports ‘record quarterly revenue’
item.description: This is Cisco's latest layoff in recent years, while the company's chief executive touts record revenue and growth.

item.title: I used Dale Carnegie’s people skills on ChatGPT — and the AI instantly became more helpful
item.description: Treating ChatGPT less like Google and more like a colleague completely changed the quality of its responses.

item.title: Wirestock raises $23M to supply creative multi-modal data to AI labs
item.description: Wirestock has over 700,000 creators on its platform, which supplies photos, videos and 3D content to AI labs.

item.title: Two weeks left: Startup Battlefield 200 applications close May 27
item.description: Your shot at VC access, global visibility, TechCrunch coverage, and $100K equity-free funding is running out. Deadline to apply is May 27. Apply now.

item.title: Ticketmaster president says queue placements aren’t randomized despite previous claims, but it’s still not giving us music fans the explanation we deserve
item.description: The president of Ticketmaster says the company doesn't randomize queue positions, and he's dug the live music giant into a massive hole.

item.title: Don't Touch the Snail is an anti-cozy game that takes permadeath to the next level
item.description: Once you die, you can never play again.

item.title: Ultrahuman Ring Pro review: The future of smart rings looks a lot like its present
item.description: The major innovations on the Ultrahuman Ring Pro are in its charging case.

item.title: Why digital sovereignty is becoming a priority for every industry
item.description: From regulation to energy costs, firms need control over data, infrastructure and AI models.

item.title: Party City store closures started a war to win over its customers. Two very different retailers are on the front lines
item.description: Michaels sells arts and craft. Staples trades in office supplies. Both retail chains want to party with you. Michaels is expanding its party supply and celebration offerings.

item.title: China’s Xi Jinping gives Trump a warning on Taiwan at Beijing summit
item.description: Back in December, Trump approved an $11 billion arms package for Taiwan, but the U.S. has yet to deliver it. Presidents Xi Jinping and Donald Trump started a crucial series of meetings in Beijing on Thursday in a U.S.-China summit where stability in the relationship is the main goal of the two days of discussions.The White House and Chinese state media said the leaders concluded their meeting Thursday morning after about two hours. Trump is expected to leave just after midday Friday after a final private meeting with Xi. But few breakthroughs are expected on divisive issues ranging from the Ir...

item.title: Uber to open 2 campuses in India to support product development, operations
item.description: Uber plans to open two new engineering campuses with a capacity of nearly 10,000 people in India by the end of 2027.

item.title: Dyson put a camera on its purifier so fresh air can follow you around the room
item.description: Dyson has announced a new air purifying bladeless fan that's its first to incorporate an AI-powered camera for tracking the location of people in a room. Dyson already uses a mix of cameras and AI in its robovacs, but since the new Find+Follow Purifier Cool is stationary and not at risk of running into abandoned […]

item.title: Embrace the Gamepad on Your Gaming PC: The Xbox Mode Guide
item.description: Xbox Mode brings a console-style experience to PC gaming with a unified library, improved performance, and controller-focused features.

item.title: The Lego The Lord of The Rings: Balrog Book Nook Kit is under $110 at Amazon — save over $20
item.description: As of May 14, the Lego Icons 'The Lord of The Rings': Balrog Book Nook Kit is on sale at Amazon for $108.99. This is 16% off its list price of $129.99.
